title: Maxillaria valenzuelana
taxonomy: Maxillaria valenzuelana is classified within the family Orchidaceae and belongs to the genus Maxillaria. The species Maxillaria valenzuelana is further categorized as a member of the subtribe Maxillariinae. Maxillariinae is a subtribe of orchids within the tribe Maxillarieae. The tribe Maxillarieae is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification is based on the current understanding of the plant's evolutionary relationships and morphological characteristics.

intro: Maxillaria valenzuelana, also known as the "Venus butterfly orchid," is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and relieving pain, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as arthritis and muscle spasms. The bioactive constituents of Maxillaria valenzuelana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
